article_title: Sales in a manufacturer picnic area
body: These changes apply to the following licences: Section 34 of the Liquor Control and Licensing Regulation is amended to authorize two types of areas under apicnic area endorsement: New Picnic Endorsement Sale Area A manufacturer may now sell and serve their products by the glass in a picnic sale area. Liquor consumption is also permitted in a sale area. Conditions for the new sale area include: All areas under the picnic area endorsement mustnot exceed1000 square metres. The conditions for the consumption-only area(s) have not changed. For example: To read all the rules for these changes, please refer to the: If you have a Temporary Expanded Service Area (TESA) Manufacturers that have a Temporary Expanded Service Area (TESA) for outdoor sampling can now apply for a picnic area endorsement to make their TESA permanent. Applications can be submitted in theliquor and cannabis licensing portal.It’s free to apply. If you have a picnic area endorsement To add a new sale area, submit a “change to an approved picnic area endorsement” application in the portal.

minutes_briefs.title: BC liquor manufacturers can now sell by the glass in picnic areas
minutes_briefs.summary: BC liquor manufacturers with a picnic area endorsement can now sell and serve their own products by the glass in a designated sale area, and customers can drink there too. The total picnic area (including both sale and consumption-only zones) must stay under 1,000 square metres. Manufacturers with a Temporary Expanded Service Area (TESA) for outdoor sampling can now apply for a permanent picnic area endorsement for free.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you have a TESA, apply for a picnic area endorsement in the liquor and cannabis licensing portal to make it permanent.
